content_en:
Khalid bin Waleed was the enemy commander as he had not yet converted to Islam; taking this opportunity he led his men by way of the mountain pass to launch a sudden atiack on the Muslims. This was such a powerful attack that the Muslims were barely able to hold their own. As a result the Mujahideen suffered a heavy loss of life. Seventy Mujahideen were martyred and the Prophet % was injured. He lost his teeth and the chains of his armour pierced his face.

Two Companions, Yaman bin Jabir and Thabit bin Wagas Wwe 4! -, who had become too old to fight in the Jiidd, were put in charge of the tents of the women and children. But when they saw their Muslim brothers attaining martyrdom they also were inspired to fight. They picked up their swords and entered the battlefield. Thabit 4 gained his goal of martyrdom. Yaman bin Jabir 4 got caught up in a circle of Mujahideen who did not know his identity and thought that he was one of the enemy. In the tumult and confusion of the battle he was wounded fatally. When Huthaifah bin Yaman + saw him he realised what had happened and called out, “Brothers this is my father! Stop!” But in all the noise of the battle no one heard him. When he saw his father’s dead body he called out in choking tones.

“My brothers, what have you done? May Allah % forgive you. Your swords have been tainted with the blood of my great father.”

All the Companions were perturbed when they saw what had happened. Suddenly everything seemed to change; though victory belonged to neither, the Muslims suffered a heavy loss of life due to

the fact that they were encircled by the enemy. After reaching AlMadinah, the Prophet 3 wished to give blood money to Huthaifah bin Yaman . But he was magnanimous even in his grief and gave the amount for the Mujahideen and said,

“O Prophet of Allah $ my father yearned to be a martyr and he achieved his desire. O Allah %, bear witness that I have dedicated this amount of the blood money for the Muslims.”

This gesture of his raised his status in the eyes of the Prophet #.

The Prophet # left Al-Madinah with an army of one thousand soldiers under him for Mount Uhud. Even before reaching his destination he had to face the treachery of the hypocrite, ‘Abdullah bin Ubbi, who left taking three hundred of his men with him. There were only seven hundred Mujahideen left under him now. Abu Sufydn was facing him with an army of three thousand armed men. Having learnt their lesson from the Battle of Badr, they had organised their ranks very well. In spite of the huge difference in numbers they launched sucha daring and powerful attack that the enemy was shaken and could not face them. The ranks of the Qureish were thrown into confusion. If only the fifty men under ‘Abdullah bin Jubair 4% had not deserted their posts, this would have been a forerunner of a decisive victory for the Muslims. But what was an error from the point of view of military strategy changed the situation in favour of the enemy. The sudden attack and the turn of events disconcerted and distressed the Muslims and they started fighting wildly and killing their own people in the ensuing confusion. The disbelievers took this to mean that it was a victory for them and they turned to go back. The Muslims returned to Al-Madinah disappointed; the Prophet 3, however, reorganised them the very next day and set out in pursuit of the enemy.

Abu Sufyan realised too late that they should have followed the Muslims to Al-Madinah and plundered and killed them in their own homes. He regretted that a great opportunity to fulfil his desire to destroy the Muslims had been lost, and felt that they should never have returned empty-handed. When he consulted with his men regarding this slip of his, they were also of the opinion that they should turn back and attack Al-Madinah. But even before they set out one of the leaders of the Banu Khaza‘ah tribe who happened to be passing that way said:

“What are you thinking about? For your information the Muslims have already organised themselves and are coming in pursuit of you people. You will see them appear any moment.”

When they realised what was happening Abu Sufyan and his disbelievers changed their dastardly plans and took off for Makkah. The fact that in spite of the numerical minority of the Muslims and the loss of lives suffered by them, the disbelievers did not dare to face them speaks volumes for the awe that they inspired.

On an examination of the Battle of Uhud it becomes clear that the factors the Prophet 3 gave weight to in his strategy - for example the defence of Mount Uhud - were crucial to winning the battle. The moment a vacuum was created on the mountain they suffered heavy losses. And it was an innovative move that was made in military history when they followed the policy of regrouping themselves the very next day to pursue the enemy. Even in modern day warfare this tactic of chasing the enemy to keep him away from their capital, is considered to be a sign of triumph. By chasing the enemy, the Prophet 3 infused the Battle of Uhud with an aura of victory.

In the Battle of the Trenches the disbelievers had trapped the Muslims resident in Al-Madinah in a lengthy siege. It was avery hard and difficult time, and the Qurasih and their allies too were not having an easy time of it. Suddenly a dust storm with swift winds broke out; the

tents of the Quraish were uprooted and torn apart, the huge tureens with cooked food were tossed upside down and lights were extinguished. There was dust everywhere - in people’s eyes, noses and throats. In seconds the might and power of the enemy was destroyed and their nefarious designs turned to dust.

From the military point of view victory is deemed to belong to the side which displays fortitude and courage at such critical moments. It is the side which starts to mourn and panic which is considered the loser.

At this moment in time the Prophet # needed the expertise and experience of Huthaifah bin Yaman 4%. He sent him to find out the actual state of affairs of the enemy. Huthaifah bin Yaman 4 disguised himself and risked his life to enter the ranks of the enemy. Of course he did not talk about this ride into the jaws of death to anyone. It is worth reading about it in his own words. He says,

“One night we were sitting in rows; the tents of Abu Sufyan were in the northern part of Af-Madinah, and the people of the Jewish tribe of Banu Quraidhah were in the south. I felt that the danger was greater from them and that they might harm our women and children. We had not seen a darker night and could not even see our own hands. Neither had we experienced a more dreadful storm. The sound and fury of the thunder and the flashes of lightening were terrifying.”

The hypocrites from the very beginning had started making excuses and asking permission to leave. They told the Prophet % that since their houses were not locked, the enemy could not only enter them and cause irreparable damage, but also harm their wives and children. But there was no such danger, and they were lying. That night the Prophet % was going around checking on each of his Mujahid to see how they were doing. When he reached where I was he could not recognise me in the darkness. Tired, cold and hungry, I was huddled in a small sheet and crouched low with my head between my knees. He asked who it was. | answered that it was his servant, Huthaifah 4.

He said, ‘Huthaifah, why are you sitting here? ‘ I answered that | was feeling exhausted with cold and hunger. He whispered to me softly,

“The enemy are in a perilous situation, go and mingle with their men and study their real position. Then come and tell me what their intentions are.”

I immediately got up on hearing his command, bat I was in fear of the enemy and was trembling from head to foot. When he saw my condition he offered the following supplication to Allah %, ‘O Lord Protect Huthaifah from the front and back, from the right and the left, from above and below.’ I swear by Allah Almighty that the moment he finished his words of prayer, I stopped feeling cold and fear of the enemy left my heart. When I left on this mission the Prophet # advised me to find out only the hidden plans of the enemy and not to take any other action under any circumstances. I moved around the enemy ranks in the darkness in sucha manner that no one could guess who I reaily was. Some time later Abu Sufyan addressed his troops and said,

‘O people of the Quraish! Today I wish to say something to you, but I fear that this may reach the ears of Muhammad %. Every individual should check who is seated on either side of him.’ As soon as I heard these words, I turned to the man seated next to me and holding his hand asked him who he was and what was his name. Thus he did not get a chance to ask me anything and my ruse worked.”

Abu Sufyan then said,

“O people of the tribe of Quraish! You will not find any peace here. The storm has killed our animals, our tents have been blown away and_ the people of the Banu Quraidhah have been separated from us. In my opinion it is better to quit this place; I myself am leaving.’ Saying this, he got on to his camel and left.”
content_id:
Khalid bin Waleed saat itu adalah komandan musuh karena ia belum masuk Islam; memanfaatkan kesempatan ini ia memimpin pasukannya melalui celah gunung untuk melancarkan serangan mendadak terhadap kaum Muslim. Serangan ini begitu dahsyat sehingga kaum Muslim nyaris tidak mampu bertahan. Akibatnya, para Mujahidin menderita kerugian jiwa yang besar. Tujuh puluh Mujahidin gugur sebagai syuhada dan Nabi ﷺ terluka. Beliau kehilangan gigi dan rantai baju besinya menusuk wajah beliau.

Dua orang sahabat, Yaman bin Jabir dan Thabit bin Waqas radhiyallahu 'anhuma, yang sudah terlalu tua untuk berperang dalam jihad, ditempatkan untuk menjaga tenda-tenda para wanita dan anak-anak. Namun ketika mereka melihat saudara-saudara Muslim mereka meraih syahadah, mereka pun terinspirasi untuk bertempur. Mereka mengambil pedang dan memasuki medan pertempuran. Thabit radhiyallahu 'anhu meraih tujuannya yaitu syahadah. Yaman bin Jabir radhiyallahu 'anhu terjebak dalam lingkaran para Mujahidin yang tidak mengenali identitasnya dan mengira bahwa ia adalah salah satu musuh. Dalam kekacauan dan kebingungan pertempuran, ia terluka parah hingga meninggal. Ketika Huthaifah bin Yaman radhiyallahu 'anhu melihatnya, ia menyadari apa yang telah terjadi dan berseru, "Saudara-saudara, ini ayahku! Berhenti!" Namun dalam hiruk-pikuk pertempuran tidak ada yang mendengarnya. Ketika ia melihat jasad ayahnya, ia berseru dengan suara yang tercekat.

"Saudara-saudaraku, apa yang telah kalian lakukan? Semoga Allah ﷻ mengampuni kalian. Pedang kalian telah ternodai darah ayahku yang mulia."

Semua sahabat merasa terguncang ketika melihat apa yang telah terjadi. Tiba-tiba segalanya tampak berubah; meskipun kemenangan tidak dimiliki oleh pihak mana pun, kaum Muslim menderita kerugian jiwa yang besar karena

mereka terkepung oleh musuh. Setelah tiba di Al-Madinah, Nabi ﷺ ingin memberikan diyat (uang darah) kepada Huthaifah bin Yaman radhiyallahu 'anhu. Namun ia bersikap mulia bahkan dalam kesedihannya dan memberikan jumlah tersebut untuk para Mujahidin seraya berkata,

"Wahai Rasulullah ﷺ, ayahku sangat mendambakan menjadi syahid dan ia telah meraih keinginannya. Ya Allah ﷻ, saksikanlah bahwa aku telah mendedikasikan jumlah diyat ini untuk kaum Muslim."

Sikap mulianya ini mengangkat kedudukannya di mata Nabi ﷺ.

Nabi ﷺ meninggalkan Al-Madinah dengan pasukan seribu prajurit di bawah pimpinan beliau menuju Gunung Uhud. Bahkan sebelum mencapai tujuan, beliau harus menghadapi pengkhianatan seorang munafik, 'Abdullah bin Ubbi, yang pergi membawa tiga ratus orang pasukannya. Kini hanya tersisa tujuh ratus Mujahidin di bawah pimpinan beliau. Abu Sufyan menghadapi beliau dengan pasukan tiga ribu orang bersenjata. Setelah mengambil pelajaran dari Perang Badr, mereka telah mengatur barisan dengan sangat baik. Meskipun terdapat perbedaan jumlah yang sangat besar, kaum Muslim melancarkan serangan yang begitu berani dan dahsyat sehingga musuh terguncang dan tidak mampu menghadapi mereka. Barisan Quraisy porak-poranda. Seandainya saja lima puluh orang di bawah 'Abdullah bin Jubair radhiyallahu 'anhu tidak meninggalkan pos mereka, ini akan menjadi awal kemenangan yang menentukan bagi kaum Muslim. Namun apa yang merupakan kesalahan dari sudut pandang strategi militer telah mengubah situasi menjadi menguntungkan musuh. Serangan mendadak dan perubahan keadaan membuat kaum Muslim bingung dan tertekan, sehingga mereka mulai bertempur secara liar dan membunuh orang-orang mereka sendiri dalam kekacauan yang terjadi. Orang-orang kafir menganggap ini sebagai kemenangan bagi mereka dan berbalik untuk kembali. Kaum Muslim kembali ke Al-Madinah dengan kecewa; namun Nabi ﷺ mengorganisasi ulang mereka keesokan harinya dan berangkat mengejar musuh.

Abu Sufyan menyadari terlambat bahwa mereka seharusnya mengikuti kaum Muslim ke Al-Madinah dan merampok serta membunuh mereka di rumah mereka sendiri. Ia menyesali bahwa kesempatan besar untuk memenuhi keinginannya menghancurkan kaum Muslim telah hilang, dan merasa bahwa mereka tidak seharusnya kembali dengan tangan kosong. Ketika ia bermusyawarah dengan pasukannya mengenai kelalaian ini, mereka juga berpendapat bahwa mereka harus berbalik dan menyerang Al-Madinah. Namun bahkan sebelum mereka berangkat, salah seorang pemimpin kabilah Banu Khuza'ah yang kebetulan melintas berkata:

"Apa yang sedang kalian pikirkan? Untuk informasi kalian, kaum Muslim telah mengorganisasi diri mereka dan sedang datang mengejar kalian. Kalian akan melihat mereka muncul kapan saja."

Ketika mereka menyadari apa yang terjadi, Abu Sufyan dan orang-orang kafirnya mengubah rencana jahat mereka dan bergegas menuju Makkah. Fakta bahwa meskipun kaum Muslim berjumlah lebih sedikit dan menderita kerugian jiwa, orang-orang kafir tidak berani menghadapi mereka menunjukkan betapa besar rasa gentar yang ditimbulkan kaum Muslim.

Dari pemeriksaan terhadap Perang Uhud, menjadi jelas bahwa faktor-faktor yang dipertimbangkan Nabi ﷺ dalam strateginya - misalnya pertahanan Gunung Uhud - sangat krusial untuk memenangkan pertempuran. Begitu kekosongan tercipta di gunung, mereka menderita kerugian besar. Dan merupakan langkah inovatif dalam sejarah militer ketika mereka mengikuti kebijakan mengorganisasi ulang diri mereka keesokan harinya untuk mengejar musuh. Bahkan dalam peperangan modern, taktik mengejar musuh untuk menjauhkannya dari ibu kota dianggap sebagai tanda kemenangan. Dengan mengejar musuh, Nabi ﷺ mewarnai Perang Uhud dengan aura kemenangan.

Dalam Perang Khandaq (Parit), orang-orang kafir telah mengepung kaum Muslim yang tinggal di Al-Madinah dalam pengepungan yang panjang. Itu adalah waktu yang sangat berat dan sulit, dan Quraisy beserta sekutu-sekutu mereka juga tidak dalam keadaan mudah. Tiba-tiba badai debu dengan angin kencang melanda;

tenda-tenda Quraisy tercabut dan robek, kuali-kuali besar berisi makanan yang sudah dimasak terbalik, dan lampu-lampu padam. Debu ada di mana-mana - di mata, hidung, dan tenggorokan orang-orang. Dalam hitungan detik, kekuatan dan kekuasaan musuh hancur dan rencana jahat mereka menjadi debu.

Dari sudut pandang militer, kemenangan dianggap milik pihak yang menunjukkan ketabahan dan keberanian di saat-saat kritis seperti itu. Pihak yang mulai meratap dan panik dianggap sebagai pihak yang kalah.

Pada saat inilah Nabi ﷺ membutuhkan keahlian dan pengalaman Huthaifah bin Yaman radhiyallahu 'anhu. Beliau mengutusnya untuk mengetahui keadaan sebenarnya dari musuh. Huthaifah bin Yaman radhiyallahu 'anhu menyamar dan mempertaruhkan nyawanya untuk masuk ke barisan musuh. Tentu saja ia tidak menceritakan tentang perjalanan ke mulut kematian ini kepada siapa pun. Sangat berharga untuk membaca tentangnya dalam kata-katanya sendiri. Ia berkata,

"Suatu malam kami duduk berbaris; tenda-tenda Abu Sufyan berada di bagian utara Al-Madinah, dan orang-orang dari kabilah Yahudi Banu Quraidhah berada di selatan. Aku merasa bahwa bahaya lebih besar dari mereka dan bahwa mereka mungkin mencelakai para wanita dan anak-anak kami. Kami belum pernah melihat malam yang lebih gelap dan bahkan tidak bisa melihat tangan kami sendiri. Kami juga belum pernah mengalami badai yang lebih mengerikan. Suara dan keganasan guntur serta kilatan petir sungguh menakutkan."

Para munafik sejak awal sudah mulai membuat alasan dan meminta izin untuk pergi. Mereka memberitahu Nabi ﷺ bahwa karena rumah-rumah mereka tidak terkunci, musuh tidak hanya bisa masuk dan menyebabkan kerusakan yang tak dapat diperbaiki, tetapi juga mencelakai istri dan anak-anak mereka. Namun tidak ada bahaya seperti itu, dan mereka berbohong. Malam itu Nabi ﷺ berkeliling memeriksa setiap Mujahidnya untuk melihat keadaan mereka. Ketika beliau sampai di tempatku, beliau tidak bisa mengenaliku dalam kegelapan. Lelah, kedinginan, dan lapar, aku meringkuk dalam selembar kain kecil dan berjongkok rendah dengan kepalaku di antara kedua lututku. Beliau bertanya siapa itu. Aku menjawab bahwa itu adalah hambanya, Huthaifah radhiyallahu 'anhu.

Beliau berkata, 'Huthaifah, mengapa kamu duduk di sini?' Aku menjawab bahwa aku merasa kelelahan karena kedinginan dan kelaparan. Beliau berbisik kepadaku dengan lembut,

"Musuh sedang dalam keadaan genting, pergilah dan berbaur dengan pasukan mereka dan pelajari posisi mereka yang sebenarnya. Kemudian kembalilah dan beritahu aku apa rencana mereka."

Aku segera berdiri mendengar perintah beliau, namun aku takut pada musuh dan gemetar dari ujung kepala hingga ujung kaki. Ketika beliau melihat keadaanku, beliau memanjatkan du'a kepada Allah ﷻ, 'Ya Rabb, lindungilah Huthaifah dari depan dan belakang, dari kanan dan kiri, dari atas dan bawah.' Aku bersumpah demi Allah Yang Maha Kuasa bahwa begitu beliau selesai mengucapkan kata-kata doanya, aku berhenti merasa kedinginan dan rasa takut pada musuh meninggalkan hatiku. Ketika aku berangkat dalam misi ini, Nabi ﷺ menasihati aku untuk hanya mencari tahu rencana tersembunyi musuh dan tidak mengambil tindakan lain dalam keadaan apa pun. Aku bergerak di antara barisan musuh dalam kegelapan sedemikian rupa sehingga tidak ada yang bisa menebak siapa aku sebenarnya. Beberapa saat kemudian Abu Sufyan berbicara kepada pasukannya dan berkata,

'Wahai kaum Quraisy! Hari ini aku ingin menyampaikan sesuatu kepada kalian, tetapi aku khawatir hal ini akan sampai ke telinga Muhammad %. Setiap orang hendaknya memeriksa siapa yang duduk di kedua sisinya.' Begitu aku mendengar kata-kata itu, aku menoleh kepada orang yang duduk di sebelahku dan sambil memegang tangannya, aku bertanya siapa dia dan apa namanya. Dengan demikian dia tidak mendapat kesempatan untuk bertanya kepadaku dan tipu dayaku berhasil."

Abu Sufyan kemudian berkata,

"Wahai kaum Quraisy! Kalian tidak akan menemukan ketenangan di sini. Badai telah membunuh hewan-hewan kita, tenda-tenda kita telah terbang tertiup angin, dan orang-orang Banu Quraidhah telah terpisah dari kita. Menurutku lebih baik meninggalkan tempat ini; aku sendiri akan pergi.' Setelah berkata demikian, dia naik ke untanya dan pergi."
content_ar: Khalid bin Waleed was the العدو القائد as he had not yet converted to الإسلام; taking this opportunity he led his men by الطريق of the mountain pass to launch a sudden atiack on the المسلمون. This was such a powerful attack that the المسلمون were barely able to hold their own. ونتيجة لذلك the المجاهدين suffered a heavy loss of الحياة. Seventy المجاهدين were الشهيدed and النبي ﷺ % was injured. He lost his teeth and the chains of his armour pierced his face. Two الصحابة, Yaman bin Jabir and Thabit bin Wagas Wwe 4! -, who had become أيضاً old to fight in the Jiidd, were put in charge of the tents of the women and الأولاد. But عندما they saw their مسلم brothers attaining الشهيدdom they أيضاً were inspired to fight. They picked up their السيفs and entered the المعركةfield. Thabit 4 gained his goal of الشهيدdom. Yaman bin Jabir 4 got caught up in a circle of المجاهدين who did not know his identity and thought that he was one of the العدو. In the tumult and confusion of the المعركة he was wounded fatally. عندما Huthaifah bin Yaman + saw him he realised what had happened and called out, “Brothers this is my الأب! Stop!” But in all the noise of the المعركة no one heard him. عندما he saw his الأب’s dead الجسد he called out in choking tones. “My brothers, what have you done? May الله % forgive you. Your السيفs have been tainted with the blood of my great الأب.” All the الصحابة were perturbed عندما they saw what had happened. Suddenly everything seemed to change; though النصر belonged to neither, the المسلمون suffered a heavy loss of الحياة بسبب the fact that they were encircled by the العدو. بعد reaching AlMadinah, النبي ﷺ 3 wished to give blood المال to Huthaifah bin Yaman . But he was magnanimous even in his الحزن and gave the amount for the المجاهدين and said, “O النبي of الله $ my الأب yearned to be a الشهيد and he achieved his desire. O الله %, bear witness that I have dedicated this amount of the blood المال for the المسلمون.” This gesture of his raised his status in the eyes of النبي ﷺ #. النبي ﷺ # left Al-المدينة المنورة with an الجيش of one thousand soldiers under him for Mount Uhud. Even قبل reaching his destination he had to face the treachery of the hypocrite, ‘Abdullah bin Ubbi, who left taking three hundred of his men with him. There were only seven hundred المجاهدين left under him now. Abu Sufydn was facing him with an الجيش of three thousand armed men. Having learnt their lesson from the المعركة of Badr, they had organised their ranks very well. In spite of the huge difference in numbers they launched sucha daring and powerful attack that the العدو was shaken and could not face them. The ranks of the Qureish were thrown into confusion. إذا only the fifty men under ‘Abdullah bin Jubair 4% had not deserted their posts, this would have been a forerunner of a decisive النصر for the المسلمون. But what was an error from the point of view of military strategy changed the situation in النعمة of the العدو. The sudden attack and the turn of events disconcerted and distressed the المسلمون and they started fighting wildly and killing their own people in the ensuing confusion. The disbelievers took this to mean that it was a النصر for them and they turned to go back. The المسلمون returned to Al-المدينة المنورة disappointed; النبي ﷺ 3, غير أن, reorganised them the very next day and set out in pursuit of the العدو. Abu Sufyan realised أيضاً late that they should have followed the المسلمون to Al-المدينة المنورة and plundered and killed them in their own homes. He regretted that a great opportunity to fulfil his desire to destroy the المسلمون had been lost, and felt that they should never have returned empty-handed. عندما he consulted with his men فيما يخص this slip of his, they were أيضاً of the opinion that they should ارتدوا and attack Al-المدينة المنورة. But even قبل they set out one of the leaders of the Banu Khaza‘ah tribe who happened to be passing that الطريق said: “What are you thinking about? For your information the المسلمون have already organised themselves and are coming in pursuit of you people. You will see them appear any moment.” عندما they realised what was happening Abu Sufyan and his disbelievers changed their dastardly plans and took off for مكة المكرمة. The fact that in spite of the numerical minority of the المسلمون and the loss of lives suffered by them, the disbelievers did not dare to face them speaks volumes for the awe that they inspired. On an examination of the المعركة of Uhud it becomes clear that the factors النبي ﷺ 3 gave weight to in his strategy - على سبيل المثال the defence of Mount Uhud - were البالغ الأهمية to winning the المعركة. The moment a vacuum was created on the mountain they suffered heavy losses. And it was an innovative move that was made in military history عندما they followed the policy of regrouping themselves the very next day to pursue the العدو. Even in modern day warfare this tactic of chasing the العدو to keep him away from their capital, is considered to be a sign of triumph. By chasing the العدو, النبي ﷺ 3 infused the المعركة of Uhud with an aura of النصر. In the المعركة of the Trenches the disbelievers had trapped the المسلمون resident in Al-المدينة المنورة in a lengthy siege. It was avery hard and difficult time, and the Qurasih and their allies أيضاً were not having an easy time of it. Suddenly a dust storm with swift winds broke out; the tents of the Quraish were uprooted and torn apart, the huge tureens with cooked food were tossed upside down and lights were extinguished. There was dust everywhere - in people’s eyes, noses and throats. In seconds the might and power of the العدو was destroyed and their nefarious designs turned to dust. From the military point of view النصر is deemed to belong to the side which displays fortitude and الشجاعة at such البالغ الأهمية moments. It is the side which starts to mourn and panic which is considered the loser. At this moment in time النبي ﷺ # needed the expertise and experience of Huthaifah bin Yaman 4%. He sent him to find out the actual state of affairs of the العدو. Huthaifah bin Yaman 4 disguised himself and risked his الحياة to enter the ranks of the العدو. Of course he did not talk about this ride into the jaws of الموت to anyone. It is worth reading about it in his own words. He says, “One night we were sitting in rows; the tents of Abu Sufyan were in the northern part of Af-المدينة المنورة, and the people of the Jewish tribe of Banu Quraidhah were in the south. I felt that the danger was greater from them and that they might harm our women and الأولاد. We had not seen a darker night and could not even see our own hands. Neither had we experienced a more dreadful storm. The sound and fury of the thunder and the flashes of lightening were terrifying.” The hypocrites from the very beginning had started making excuses and asking permission to leave. They told النبي ﷺ % that بما أن their houses were not locked, the العدو could not only enter them and cause irreparable damage, but أيضاً harm their wives and الأولاد. But there was no such danger, and they were lying. That night النبي ﷺ % was going around checking on each of his Mujahid to see how they were doing. عندما he reached where I was he could not recognise me in the darkness. Tired, cold and hungry, I was huddled in a small sheet and crouched low with my head between my knees. He asked who it was. | answered that it was his servant, Huthaifah 4. قال, ‘Huthaifah, why are you sitting here? ‘ I answered that | was feeling exhausted with cold and hunger. He whispered to me softly, “The العدو are in a perilous situation, go and mingle with their men and study their real position. Then come and tell me what their intentions are.” I immediately got up on hearing his command, bat I was in الخوف of the العدو and was trembling from head to foot. عندما he saw my condition he offered the following الدعاء to الله %, ‘O Lord Protect Huthaifah from the front and back, from the الحق and the left, from above and below.’ I swear by الله Almighty that the moment he finished his words of الصلاة, I stopped feeling cold and الخوف of the العدو left my القلب. عندما I left on this mission النبي ﷺ # advised me to find out only the hidden plans of the العدو and not to take any other action under any circumstances. I moved around the العدو ranks in the darkness in sucha manner that no one could guess who I reaily was. Some time later Abu Sufyan addressed his القوات and said, ‘O people of the Quraish! Today I wish to say something to you, but I الخوف that this may reach the ears of محمد ﷺ %. Every individual should check who is seated on either side of him.’ As soon as I heard these words, I turned to the man seated next to me and holding his hand asked him who he was and what was his name. وهكذا he did not get a chance to ask me anything and my ruse worked.” Abu Sufyan then said, “O people of the tribe of Quraish! You will not find any peace here. The storm has killed our animals, our tents have been blown away and_ the people of the Banu Quraidhah have been separated from us. In my opinion it is better to quit this place; I myself am leaving.’ Saying this, he got on to his camel and left.”
